title: Coprimality of Random Integers
description: The probability that two randomly chosen integers are coprime is $\frac{6}{\pi^2}$.

title: Imaginary Exponentiation Identity
description: Since $i^{\,i} = e^{-\pi/2}$, raising to the power $\frac{1}{\pi}$ gives $e^{-1/2}$, and inverting squared yields $e$.

title: Infinite Nested Radical
description: An infinite power tower of $\sqrt[x]{x}$ converges to $x$.

title: Digital Root Congruence
description: For any integer $n$, the number itself is congruent to the sum of its digits modulo 9.

title: Intercollegiate Math Tournament 2026 Archive
description: A list of problems to challenge yourself for math competitions.

title: Option Pricing Explained | No Arbitrage + Financial Mathematics from a Quant
description: In this lecture, Susquehanna educator Doug Costa (former head of quant research and former University of Virginia math professor) explains no arbitrage, why arbitrage-free conditions lead to pricing formulas, and how this principle underpins derivatives pricing and models like Black-Scholes.
